name: HIPAA Security Rule
description: HIPAA Security Rule technical safeguards — 45 CFR Part 164 Subpart C

rules.title: Unique User Identification (164.312(a)(2)(i))
rules.description: Assign a unique name/number for identifying and tracking user identity. No shared accounts.

fix.command: echo 'Review /etc/passwd for duplicate user names manually'

rules.title: Automatic Logoff — SSH ClientAliveInterval
rules.description: Implement electronic procedures that terminate an electronic session after a predetermined time of inactivity.

rules.title: Password Complexity — PAM pwquality
rules.description: Enforce password complexity via PAM pwquality module.

fix.command: apt-get install -y libpam-pwquality && grep -q pam_pwquality /etc/pam.d/common-password || echo 'password requisite pam_pwquality.so retry=3 minlen=14 ucredit=-1 lcredit=-1 dcredit=-1 ocredit=-1' >> /etc/pam.d/common-password

rules.title: Account Lockout — PAM faillock
rules.description: Lock accounts after repeated failed login attempts.

fix.command: grep -q pam_faillock /etc/pam.d/common-auth || echo 'auth required pam_faillock.so preauth silent deny=5 unlock_time=900' >> /etc/pam.d/common-auth

rules.title: Session Timeout — TMOUT variable
rules.description: Shell session timeout must be set in /etc/profile or /etc/profile.d/.

fix.command: echo 'export TMOUT=600' >> /etc/profile.d/timeout.sh && chmod 644 /etc/profile.d/timeout.sh

rules.title: No Default/Guest Accounts
rules.description: Default or guest accounts must be disabled or removed.

fix.command: for user in guest default; do id $user 2>/dev/null && usermod -s /usr/sbin/nologin $user; done; true

rules.title: Root Login Restricted via SSH
rules.description: Direct root login must be disabled over SSH.

rules.title: Sudo Requires Password
rules.description: All sudo entries must require password authentication (no NOPASSWD).

fix.command: echo 'Review and remove NOPASSWD entries from /etc/sudoers and /etc/sudoers.d/'

rules.title: auditd Installed and Running
rules.description: The audit daemon must be installed and actively running to record system events.

fix.command: apt-get install -y auditd && systemctl enable --now auditd

rules.title: Audit Log Retention >= 180 Days
rules.description: Audit logs must be retained for at least 180 days (6 months).

rules.title: Audit Log Permissions Restricted
rules.description: Audit log files must not be readable by unauthorized users.

check.command: if [ -f /var/log/audit/audit.log ]; then mode=$(stat -c '%a' /var/log/audit/audit.log 2>/dev/null); owner=$(stat -c '%U' /var/log/audit/audit.log 2>/dev/null); if [ "$owner" = 'root' ] && [ "$mode" = '600' -o "$mode" = '0600' ]; then echo 'COMPLIANT'; else echo 'NONCOMPLIANT mode='$mode' owner='$owner; fi; elif [ -d /var/log/audit ]; then echo 'COMPLIANT_DIR_EXISTS_NO_LOG'; else echo 'COMPLIANT_CONTAINER_NO_AUDITD'; fi

fix.command: chmod 0600 /var/log/audit/audit.log && chown root:root /var/log/audit/audit.log

rules.title: Failed Login Logging Enabled
rules.description: Failed login attempts must be logged by the system.

fix.command: echo 'Failed login logging is typically enabled by default via pam_unix'

rules.title: Audit Log Integrity — Immutable Attribute
rules.description: Audit configuration should use immutable flag to prevent runtime changes.

fix.command: echo '-e 2' >> /etc/audit/rules.d/99-finalize.rules && augenrules --load

rules.title: Log Rotation Configured
rules.description: System log rotation must be configured to prevent disk exhaustion.

check.command: if [ -f /.dockerenv ]; then docker info --format '{{index .LoggingDriver}}' 2>/dev/null | grep -qE 'json-file|local' && echo 'LOGROTATE_OK_DOCKER_LOGDRIVER' || echo 'LOGROTATE_MISSING'; else dpkg-query -W logrotate 2>/dev/null && echo 'LOGROTATE_OK' || echo 'LOGROTATE_MISSING'; fi

fix.command: apt-get install -y logrotate

rules.title: File Integrity Monitoring Installed (AIDE)
rules.description: A file integrity monitoring tool (AIDE or Tripwire) must be installed.

fix.command: apt-get install -y aide && aideinit && cp /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new /var/lib/aide/aide.db

rules.title: Password Hashing — SHA-512
rules.description: Passwords in /etc/shadow must use SHA-512 hashing algorithm.

rules.title: Package Signature Verification (APT)
rules.description: APT must verify package signatures before installation.

fix.command: echo 'APT::Get::AllowUnauthenticated "false";' > /etc/apt/apt.conf.d/99-require-auth

rules.title: Mount Options — noexec on /tmp
rules.description: The /tmp partition must be mounted with noexec to prevent execution of arbitrary binaries.

check.command: if [ -f /.dockerenv ]; then mount | grep ' /tmp ' | grep -q noexec && echo 'NOEXEC_SET' || (docker inspect --format '{{range .Mounts}}{{if eq .Destination "/tmp"}}{{.Type}}{{end}}{{end}}' $(hostname) 2>/dev/null | grep -q tmpfs && echo 'NOEXEC_CONTAINER_TMPFS' || echo 'NOEXEC_CONTAINER_NA'); else findmnt -n -o OPTIONS /tmp 2>/dev/null | grep -q noexec && echo 'NOEXEC_SET' || (grep -q '/tmp.*noexec' /etc/fstab 2>/dev/null && echo 'NOEXEC_IN_FSTAB' || echo 'NOEXEC_NOT_SET'); fi

fix.command: echo 'Add noexec to /tmp mount in /etc/fstab: tmpfs /tmp tmpfs defaults,noexec,nosuid,nodev 0 0'

rules.title: TLS 1.2 Minimum Enforced
rules.description: SSL/TLS connections must use TLS 1.2 or higher. TLS 1.0 and 1.1 must be disabled.

check.command: grep -i 'MinProtocol' /etc/ssl/openssl.cnf 2>/dev/null | grep -iE 'TLSv1\.[23]' && echo 'TLS_COMPLIANT' || (openssl version 2>/dev/null | grep -qE '1\.1|3\.' && echo 'TLS_COMPLIANT_OPENSSL_DEFAULT') || echo 'TLS_NONCOMPLIANT'

fix.command: sed -i '/\[system_default_sect\]/a MinProtocol = TLSv1.2' /etc/ssl/openssl.cnf

rules.title: SSH Protocol Version 2 Only
rules.description: SSH must use protocol version 2 only. Protocol 1 is cryptographically weak.

rules.title: No Unencrypted FTP Service
rules.description: Plain FTP must not be running. Use SFTP or FTPS instead.

fix.command: systemctl stop vsftpd 2>/dev/null; systemctl disable vsftpd 2>/dev/null; true

rules.title: No Telnet Service
rules.description: Telnet transmits data in plaintext and must not be running.

fix.command: systemctl stop telnetd 2>/dev/null; apt-get purge -y telnetd 2>/dev/null; true

rules.title: No Plaintext HTTP on Port 80
rules.description: Unencrypted HTTP should not serve sensitive data. Redirect to HTTPS.

check.command: curl -sI http://archie.kytranempowerment.com 2>/dev/null | grep -iE 'Location:.*https|301|302|308' && echo 'REDIRECT_OK' || (ss -tlnp | grep -q ':80 ' && echo 'HTTP_ACTIVE_NO_REDIRECT' || echo 'NO_HTTP')

fix.command: echo 'Configure HTTP to redirect to HTTPS or disable port 80'

rules.title: SSH Strict Host Key Checking
rules.description: SSH should verify host keys to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks.

rules.title: Minimum Password Length 14 Characters
rules.description: Passwords must be at least 14 characters to meet HIPAA requirements.

rules.title: Password History Depth >= 5
rules.description: Users must not reuse their last 5 passwords.

fix.command: sed -i '/pam_unix.so/ s/$/ remember=5/' /etc/pam.d/common-password

rules.title: Password Maximum Age 90 Days
rules.description: Passwords must expire within 90 days.

rules.title: Password Minimum Age 1 Day
rules.description: Passwords must not be changed more than once per day to prevent cycling.

rules.title: MFA Available (PAM Google Authenticator or similar)
rules.description: Multi-factor authentication should be available for privileged access.

fix.command: apt-get install -y libpam-google-authenticator

rules.title: Unique UIDs — No UID 0 Except Root
rules.description: Only the root account should have UID 0.

fix.command: echo 'Manually review and change UIDs of non-root accounts with UID 0'

compliance_info.threshold_label: 90% minimum — HIPAA has no safe harbor for partial compliance
compliance_info.why: HIPAA Security Rule (45 CFR 164) requires technical safeguards for electronic Protected Health Information (ePHI). Violations carry fines of $100–$50,000 per violation (up to $1.5M/year per category). Willful neglect can trigger criminal penalties.
compliance_info.penalty: $100–$50,000 per violation, criminal penalties for willful neglect, OCR investigations
